channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: AI Policy Lead at J-PAL (MIT): Shape the Future of AI for Social Good
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Are you passionate about using artificial intelligence to drive meaningful social impact? The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) is hiring an AI Policy Lead to support its expanding work on AI for social good. This exciting opportunity is based at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States, and offers […]

The post AI Policy Lead at J-PAL (MIT): Shape the Future of AI for Social Good appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about using artificial intelligence to drive meaningful social impact? The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) is hiring an AI Policy Lead to support its expanding work on AI for social good.

This exciting opportunity is based at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States, and offers a hybrid work structure.

About the Role

The AI Policy Lead will play a key role at the intersection of technology, research, and policymaking. This position focuses on ensuring that AI tools are developed, evaluated, and implemented in ways that are evidence-based and socially impactful.

Working within J-PAL’s Policy & Communications team, the successful candidate will help shape how AI is used to address global development challenges.

Key Responsibilities

The AI Policy Lead will be responsible for driving initiatives that connect cutting-edge research with real-world policy applications.

Core responsibilities include:

Leading efforts to evaluate and scale promising AI tools
Building partnerships with governments, researchers, and technology companies
Ensuring AI adoption decisions are grounded in rigorous, credible evidence
Supporting policy engagement and communication strategies
Contributing to research-driven policymaking efforts

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working across sectors and translating research into practical solutions.

Why This Role Matters

Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming industries and societies. Ensuring that AI is used responsibly and effectively—especially in development contexts—is critical.

This role provides a unique opportunity to influence how AI is applied to solve real-world problems, particularly in areas such as poverty reduction, public policy, and global development.

Benefits of the Role

Working at J-PAL offers a chance to collaborate with leading researchers and policymakers around the world.

Benefits include:

Exposure to cutting-edge AI and policy work
Opportunity to influence global development outcomes
Collaboration with top researchers and institutions
Hybrid work environment
Professional growth in a globally recognized research organization

Who Should Apply

This role is ideal for professionals with a strong interest in AI, public policy, and social impact.

Candidates who are passionate about bridging technology and policy, and who thrive in collaborative, research-driven environments, are encouraged to apply.

Application Details

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible.

Apply here

For more Policy Opportunities from OFY, Click here.

The post AI Policy Lead at J-PAL (MIT): Shape the Future of AI for Social Good appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026: Transform Your Impact in Climate Leadership
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026, a high-impact leadership programme designed to equip emerging climate leaders with the skills needed to navigate complex systems and drive meaningful change. This initiative is led by Common Purpose India in partnership with YES Foundation, with venue support from Samagata Foundation. […]

The post India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026: Transform Your Impact in Climate Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026, a high-impact leadership programme designed to equip emerging climate leaders with the skills needed to navigate complex systems and drive meaningful change.

This initiative is led by Common Purpose India in partnership with YES Foundation, with venue support from Samagata Foundation.

As climate challenges become increasingly central to economic growth, public health, and social equity, this programme focuses on strengthening leadership capacity rather than technical knowledge—empowering participants to lead effectively in high-pressure, resource-constrained environments.

About the Programme

The ILACL is a 4-day, in-person leadership experience designed for individuals working across climate-related sectors.

Held in Bangalore, India, the programme brings together a diverse cohort of leaders tackling challenges in areas such as biodiversity, clean air, water, waste management, and climate innovation.

Rather than focusing on technical training, the programme emphasizes leadership development, collaboration, and systems thinking.

What You Will Gain

Participants will engage in a structured learning journey aimed at strengthening their leadership effectiveness.

Key learning outcomes include:

Developing self-awareness and clarity of purpose
Building the ability to lead across sectors and diverse stakeholder groups
Strengthening influence within complex systems
Learning from experienced social sector leaders through curated sessions
Engaging in peer learning, reflection, and real-time application

The programme is highly interactive, combining workshops, discussions, and practical exercises to ensure deep learning.

Programme Benefits

ILACL offers a unique opportunity for climate professionals to grow both personally and professionally.

Benefits include:

Immersive in-person leadership training
Access to experienced mentors and sector leaders
Peer-to-peer learning with a diverse cohort
Post-programme mentoring and alumni engagement opportunities
Enhanced leadership capacity for real-world impact

This experience is particularly valuable for those looking to step into greater leadership roles within the climate and sustainability space.

Programme Details

Dates: May 26 – May 29, 2026
Location: Bangalore, India
Format: In-person (4-day intensive programme)

The programme also includes follow-up mentoring and opportunities to reconnect with the alumni network.

Who Should Apply

This programme is ideal for high-potential individuals working in climate-related fields who are ready to take on greater leadership responsibilities.

It is especially suited for professionals working in:

Climate solutions and sustainability
Environmental policy and advocacy
Urban and rural development
Social impact and nonprofit sectors

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official link .

For more Leadership opportunities, visit OFY here.

 

The post India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026: Transform Your Impact in Climate Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Smithsonian National Postal Museum Paid Summer Internships 2026: Strategy, Finance & Museum Operations Experience
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for paid summer internships at the Smithsonian National Postal Museum, part of the globally recognized Smithsonian Institution. These internships offer a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in museum operations, strategy, finance, and administration while working in Washington, D.C., United States. About the Internship Program The […]

The post Smithsonian National Postal Museum Paid Summer Internships 2026: Strategy, Finance & Museum Operations Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for paid summer internships at the Smithsonian National Postal Museum, part of the globally recognized Smithsonian Institution.

These internships offer a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in museum operations, strategy, finance, and administration while working in Washington, D.C., United States.

About the Internship Program

The Smithsonian National Postal Museum provides immersive internship experiences designed to give participants direct exposure to how a national museum operates behind the scenes.

Interns will work alongside professionals across departments, contributing to real projects in planning, operations, and internal systems development.

This program is ideal for individuals interested in museum studies, nonprofit management, business operations, and public sector careers.

Internship Tracks

Strategic Planning Internship

The Strategic Planning Intern will work closely with the museum’s executive team and gain insight into high-level decision-making and operational planning.

Key responsibilities include:

Supporting strategic planning and management processes
Conducting archival research and industry benchmarking
Learning about museum leadership and operations

Dates: June 22, 2026 – August 14, 2026

Eligibility:
Open to undergraduate students and recent graduates (within 12 months of graduation) with an interest in:

Museum studies
Nonprofit management
Business and fundraising
Arts management
History or government

Finance & Administration Internship

This role focuses on operational and administrative support within the museum’s Finance and Administration department.

Key responsibilities include:

Supporting internal operations and process improvements
Collaborating across departments
Assisting with the development of an internal SharePoint system for document management

Dates: June 8, 2026 – August 14, 2026

Eligibility:
Open to undergraduate students and recent graduates (within 6 months of graduation) with an interest in:

Museum studies
Business administration or management
Project management
Human resources

Internship Benefits

Participants will gain valuable professional experience in a world-renowned institution.

Benefits include:

Paid internship opportunity
Hands-on experience in museum operations and management
Exposure to executive-level planning and organizational strategy
Practical skills in research, administration, and collaboration
Networking opportunities within the Smithsonian community

Location

All internships are in-person in Washington, D.C., providing a fully immersive experience within the museum environment.

How to Apply

Learn more.

Apply via SOLAA (Program ID 2235).

For more Internship opportunities from OFY, Click here.

The post Smithsonian National Postal Museum Paid Summer Internships 2026: Strategy, Finance & Museum Operations Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Heidelberg Laureate Forum 2026 Journalist Travel Grant (Fully Funded to Germany)
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Heidelberg Laureate Forum Foundation is offering an exceptional opportunity for science and technology journalists worldwide to attend the 13th Heidelberg Laureate Forum (HLF) in Germany from September 13–18, 2026. This prestigious event gathers winners of the Fields Medal, Turing Award, and Abel Prize, along with 200 of the world’s most outstanding young researchers in […]

The post Heidelberg Laureate Forum 2026 Journalist Travel Grant (Fully Funded to Germany) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Heidelberg Laureate Forum Foundation is offering an exceptional opportunity for science and technology journalists worldwide to attend the 13th Heidelberg Laureate Forum (HLF) in Germany from September 13–18, 2026.

This prestigious event gathers winners of the Fields Medal, Turing Award, and Abel Prize, along with 200 of the world’s most outstanding young researchers in mathematics and computer science. It provides a unique platform for journalists to engage directly with leading experts shaping the future of science.

🌟 About the Heidelberg Laureate Forum

The Heidelberg Laureate Forum is a globally recognized networking conference that fosters dialogue between top-tier researchers and laureates in mathematics and computer science.

Journalists selected for the program gain exclusive access to:

  • In-depth discussions on cutting-edge research

  • Direct interactions with globally recognized laureates

  • Insights into emerging scientific trends

  • A vibrant international network of experts and communicators

🎤 Special Highlight

A key highlight of the 2026 forum is the Lindau Lecture, which will be delivered by Nobel Prize-winning physicist Brian Schmidt, renowned for discovering the accelerating expansion of the universe.

💰 Travel Grant Benefits

Successful applicants will receive full financial support, including:

  • Round-trip travel to Germany

  • Accommodation throughout the forum

  • Visa costs fully covered

  • Full access to all sessions and networking opportunities

This ensures participants can focus entirely on storytelling, learning, and professional growth.

📝 Application Process

To apply for the travel grant:

  1. Create an account on the official application portal

  2. Enter your personal details (name, email, login credentials)

  3. Activate your account via the confirmation email

  4. Log in and complete your application form

  5. Submit before the deadline

Ensure your application clearly demonstrates your experience in science or technology journalism and your motivation for attending.

⏰ Application Deadline

The application period runs from March 2 to April 8, 2026.

Final Deadline: April 8, 2026

Applications submitted after this date will not be considered.

🚀 Why You Should Apply

This opportunity offers immense value for journalists looking to elevate their careers:

  • Access to exclusive, high-level scientific discussions

  • Opportunities to produce impactful global stories

  • Networking with top scientists and fellow journalists

  • Exposure to groundbreaking developments in math and computer science

🔗 How to Apply

Applicants should visit the official Heidelberg Laureate Forum website, create an account, and complete the application before the deadline.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more travel grant opportunities, click here. 

The post Heidelberg Laureate Forum 2026 Journalist Travel Grant (Fully Funded to Germany) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UN Women JPO Programme Analyst Role in Malawi (Sponsored by Finland) – Launch Your Global Career in Gender Equality
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Are you passionate about advancing gender equality and empowering women worldwide? The Government of Finland is offering an incredible opportunity for young professionals to join UN Women as a Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme Analyst in Lilongwe, Malawi. This prestigious role places you at the forefront of global development work, helping shape impactful programmes that […]

The post UN Women JPO Programme Analyst Role in Malawi (Sponsored by Finland) – Launch Your Global Career in Gender Equality appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about advancing gender equality and empowering women worldwide? The Government of Finland is offering an incredible opportunity for young professionals to join UN Women as a Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme Analyst in Lilongwe, Malawi. This prestigious role places you at the forefront of global development work, helping shape impactful programmes that uplift women economically and socially.

📌 About the Opportunity

The UN Women JPO Programme Analyst (P2 level) position focuses on Women’s Economic Empowerment (WEE), a key priority area in Malawi. As part of this role, you will contribute to designing, implementing, and evaluating programmes that promote income security, decent work, and economic autonomy for women.

Working closely with UN Women teams, government stakeholders, and international partners, you’ll help drive initiatives in areas such as:

  • Entrepreneurship and skills development

  • Digital inclusion and ICT for women

  • Agricultural value chains and employment creation

  • Policy development and gender equality frameworks

This is a full-time, fixed-term position for 1 year, with the possibility of extension.

📍 Location

Lilongwe, Malawi

🎯 Key Responsibilities

As a Programme Analyst, you will:

  • Support programme design, implementation, and evaluation

  • Monitor results using Results-Based Management tools

  • Provide technical assistance and capacity building to partners

  • Coordinate programme activities and partnerships

  • Contribute to reporting, budgeting, and donor engagement

  • Support advocacy and knowledge-building initiatives

🎓 Eligibility Criteria

To be considered, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Master’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., economics, social sciences, gender studies, international development)

  • At least 2 years of relevant professional experience

  • Strong analytical, communication, and programme management skills

  • Fluency in English (additional UN languages are an asset)

🌐 Why This Opportunity Matters

Malawi presents a unique development context where women make up over half the population and contribute significantly to agriculture and the workforce—yet face systemic barriers in land ownership, finance, and leadership.

By joining this programme, you will:

  • Gain hands-on international development experience

  • Work on impactful gender equality initiatives

  • Build a global professional network

  • Enhance your leadership and policy analysis skills

📅 Application Deadline

April 16, 2026 (23:59 CET)

🔗 Application Link

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more job opportunities, click here. 

🚀 Final Thoughts

This JPO position is more than just a job—it’s a gateway to a meaningful international career. If you are driven by purpose and ready to make a difference in women’s lives globally, this is your chance to step into a transformative role with UN Women.

The post UN Women JPO Programme Analyst Role in Malawi (Sponsored by Finland) – Launch Your Global Career in Gender Equality appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for WHO Climate and SRH Research Grant — Up to $55,000 for Community-Based Projects
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you part of a research team working at the intersection of climate change and public health? The World Health Organization (WHO), through its Human Reproduction Programme (HRP), is inviting applications for a global call for research proposals focused on climate change and sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRH). This opportunity supports research teams […]

The post Apply for WHO Climate and SRH Research Grant — Up to $55,000 for Community-Based Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you part of a research team working at the intersection of climate change and public health? The World Health Organization (WHO), through its Human Reproduction Programme (HRP), is inviting applications for a global call for research proposals focused on climate change and sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRH).

This opportunity supports research teams in low- and middle-income countries to generate community-driven evidence on how climate change impacts SRH outcomes. With funding of up to USD 55,000 per project, this call is designed to strengthen research capacity while addressing urgent global health challenges through participatory and interdisciplinary approaches.

About the Programme

This call for proposals focuses on understanding how climate-related events — such as droughts, floods, and extreme temperatures — affect key areas of sexual and reproductive health and rights. These include maternal health, gender-based violence, access to contraception, and abortion care.

Selected research teams will participate in a collaborative, cross-country research initiative. Rather than submitting a finalized protocol, applicants are asked to propose a research vision that demonstrates their approach, expertise, and connection to the communities they aim to study.

A central component of the programme is a Protocol Development Workshop, scheduled to take place in Geneva in July 2026. During this workshop, selected teams will co-design a unified research framework and methodology. Travel and accommodation costs for up to two team members will be covered by WHO.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Climate change is increasingly recognized as a major global health challenge, yet its impact on sexual and reproductive health remains under-researched. This call aims to address critical evidence gaps while empowering local researchers and communities.

By supporting participatory, community-led research, the programme ensures that those most affected by climate change play an active role in shaping solutions. This approach not only improves research quality but also enhances policy relevance and real-world impact.

For research teams, this opportunity offers a pathway to contribute to global knowledge, influence policy, and strengthen long-term research capacity.

What Participants Will Gain

This opportunity provides both financial and institutional support to research teams working on critical global health issues.

Selected teams will receive up to USD 55,000 in research funding, which can be used to support personnel, community engagement activities, data collection, and dissemination efforts.

Beyond funding, participants will gain access to capacity-building resources, including training, technical guidance, and research tools provided by WHO. This support helps strengthen research design, implementation, and analysis capabilities.

Teams will also benefit from international collaboration, working alongside other selected research groups across different countries. This cross-country exchange allows for shared learning, comparative analysis, and broader research impact.

Another key benefit is access to WHO expertise and networks. Selected teams will receive guidance on ethics approval processes, data collection strategies, and dissemination, as well as connections to WHO country offices.

Additionally, the programme supports participatory research approaches, meaning community members are actively involved as co-researchers. This enhances the relevance and impact of the research while strengthening community engagement.

Who Can Apply

This call is open to research teams based in low- and middle-income countries, particularly those in priority countries identified by WHO.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Academic and research institutions
  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Community-based organizations and civil society groups
  • Multi-institutional research consortia

The Principal Investigator and core research team must be based in an eligible country. While international collaboration is encouraged, high-income country partners cannot lead the project and may receive no more than 15% of the total grant.

Teams should demonstrate:

  • Experience in SRH or climate-related research
  • Expertise in participatory and mixed-methods approaches
  • Strong community partnerships
  • Capacity to conduct interdisciplinary research

Research Focus Areas

Applicants must select one of four priority research questions:

  • Maternal health outcomes in the context of climate change
  • Gender-based violence during climate-related events
  • Access to contraception during climate shocks
  • Access to abortion care in climate-affected settings

All proposals must apply a human rights-based and intersectional approach, considering how factors such as gender, socioeconomic status, and migration influence vulnerability.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants must submit their proposals through the official online platform, including all required documents such as budget, CVs, and conflict of interest declarations.

Application Deadline: 12 April 2026 (23:59 GMT+1)

Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted. Selected teams will be notified by 11 May 2026.

 Learn more and apply through the official WHO/HRP application platform.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply for WHO Climate and SRH Research Grant — Up to $55,000 for Community-Based Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations IFAD Internship Programme(Paid Internship open to various nationalities)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is an international financial institution and a specialized United Nations agency dedicated to eradicating rural poverty and hunger. It does so by investing in rural people. IFAD finances programmes and projects that increase agricultural productivity and raise rural incomes, and advocates at the local, national and international level […]

The post United Nations IFAD Internship Programme(Paid Internship open to various nationalities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is an international financial institution and a specialized United Nations agency dedicated to eradicating rural poverty and hunger. It does so by investing in rural people. IFAD finances programmes and projects that increase agricultural productivity and raise rural incomes, and advocates at the local, national and international level for policies that contribute to rural transformation.

Job Role

The regular Internship Programme (IP) and Enhanced Internship Programme (EIP) offer a unique learning experience of six months to recent university graduates or students who are currently specializing in areas of work relevant to IFAD’s mission. Candidates for the Internship Programme must be 30 years old or less and must be enrolled in an accredited university or graduate school, have attended courses in the last 12 months, and have completed at least two years of undergraduate studies when joining the programme.

Candidates must meet the following criteria:

  • be enrolled in a university or graduate school, have attended courses in the last 12 months, and have completed at least two years of undergraduate studies when joining IFAD through the programme or;
  • have recently completed university studies at the undergraduate or postgraduate level;
  • be fluent in English; fluency in other IFAD official languages is an asset. In the case of interns recruited in IFAD country office, multi country office or regional office locations, fluency in the main country language may be required;
  • be 30 years of age or younger. The Enhanced Internship Programme, in particular, is designed to enhance geographic distribution of staff at IFAD and is open to candidates who meet the above eligibility criteria and who are not based in Rome.

Benefits include:

  • Monthly stipend
  • Housing and travel allowances (Enhanced Internship Programme)
  • Mentorship, learning, and growth opportunities in a dynamic, global environment

Financial support: In addition to a monthly allowance, Enhanced Internship Programme interns receive a housing and travel allowance.

To apply, please login or register as an intern in IFAD’s applicant system. Then click on ‘View all jobs’ and select the option under Job Opening Type, Internship Programme. For more info visit: https://www.ifad.org/en/internship-programme 
FAQS
You may also be interested in this : Click here for Geospatial Analysis Internship at IFAD in Rome 

The post United Nations IFAD Internship Programme(Paid Internship open to various nationalities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PAYA Youth Council 2026: Paid Leadership Opportunity for Youth Apprentices in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the PAYA Youth Council, a paid leadership program under the Partnership to Advance Youth Apprenticeship (PAYA). This unique opportunity is designed for current and former youth apprentices who are passionate about advocacy, leadership, and expanding access to apprenticeship opportunities across the United States. Participants will gain hands-on experience leading projects, […]

The post PAYA Youth Council 2026: Paid Leadership Opportunity for Youth Apprentices in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the PAYA Youth Council, a paid leadership program under the Partnership to Advance Youth Apprenticeship (PAYA).

This unique opportunity is designed for current and former youth apprentices who are passionate about advocacy, leadership, and expanding access to apprenticeship opportunities across the United States.

Participants will gain hands-on experience leading projects, influencing policy conversations, and working alongside peers from across the country—all while receiving a stipend.

About the PAYA Youth Council

The PAYA Youth Council is a leadership development initiative that empowers young people to take an active role in shaping the future of apprenticeship programs.

Council members collaborate on youth-led projects, contribute to national conversations about workforce development, and advocate for improved access to career pathways.

The program also creates a strong sense of community, bringing together young leaders committed to making a difference.

What You Will Do

As a member of the Youth Council, participants will:

Develop advocacy and leadership skills
Lead and contribute to youth-driven projects
Engage in discussions on apprenticeship and workforce development
Collaborate with peers from across the United States
Represent youth voices in national conversations

Participants will also have opportunities to share their ideas and create impact through real-world initiatives.

Program Benefits

The PAYA Youth Council offers both professional development and financial support.

Benefits include:

A generous stipend for participation
Hands-on leadership and advocacy experience
Networking opportunities with peers and professionals
Participation in a summer celebration event in Washington, D.C.
Exposure to national apprenticeship initiatives

This is a valuable opportunity for young people looking to build leadership experience while contributing to meaningful change.

Why You Should Apply

If you are passionate about youth empowerment, workforce development, and creating opportunities for others, this program provides a platform to make your voice heard.

Past participants have highlighted the impact of seeing their work contribute to real change and helping others access opportunities.

Additional Opportunities

Interested applicants can also:

Attend an upcoming virtual info session to learn more about the program
Explore past Youth Council projects
Hear directly from former members about their experiences

These resources provide insight into what to expect and how to make the most of the opportunity.

How to Apply

Apply here

Info session

Past projects

Hear from past members

For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY Here.

The post PAYA Youth Council 2026: Paid Leadership Opportunity for Youth Apprentices in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: MSc in Public Policy and Human Development 2026: Study Policy, Development, and Global Change in the Netherlands(Scholarship Available)
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Master of Science in Public Policy and Human Development (MPP), a prestigious one-year postgraduate programme offered through a collaboration between United Nations University (UNU-MERIT) and Maastricht University. Delivered in Maastricht, Netherlands, this internationally recognized programme is designed for individuals who want to tackle complex global challenges and contribute to […]

The post MSc in Public Policy and Human Development 2026: Study Policy, Development, and Global Change in the Netherlands(Scholarship Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Master of Science in Public Policy and Human Development (MPP), a prestigious one-year postgraduate programme offered through a collaboration between United Nations University (UNU-MERIT) and Maastricht University.

Delivered in Maastricht, Netherlands, this internationally recognized programme is designed for individuals who want to tackle complex global challenges and contribute to shaping effective public policies aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals.

About the Programme

The MSc in Public Policy and Human Development is an intensive, full-time programme that runs for one year and awards two diplomas—one from Maastricht University and one from the United Nations University.

The programme combines theoretical knowledge with practical skills, preparing students to design, implement, monitor, and evaluate public policies across sectors.

With a strong focus on real-world impact, students gain both quantitative and qualitative skills needed to influence policy decisions at local, national, and global levels.

Programme Structure

The programme is divided into two main phases:

Autumn Semester
Focuses on quantitative skills such as statistics, regression analysis, and the use of tools like STATA. This phase equips students with analytical skills essential for policy research.

Spring Semester
Centers on specialization and thesis writing, allowing students to tailor their learning based on their interests.

Students can choose from four specialization tracks:

Social Protection Policy
Migration Studies
Governance of Innovation
Global Governance for Development

The programme uses Problem-Based Learning, encouraging critical thinking, collaboration, and active participation.

What You Will Learn

Participants will develop the ability to:

Analyze complex societal challenges
Design and evaluate effective public policies
Apply data-driven approaches to decision-making
Develop solutions aligned with sustainable development goals
Communicate policy recommendations effectively

The programme also includes workshops, simulations, debates, and networking events with global experts.

Programme Benefits

Students benefit from a highly international and personalized learning experience.

Key advantages include:

Dual degree from two globally recognized institutions
Small class sizes and personalized academic support
Access to global networks and policy experts
A dedicated employability track with mentoring and career support
Strong career prospects in international organizations, governments, NGOs, and private sector institutions

Graduates have gone on to work with organizations such as international development banks, UN agencies, consulting firms, and government ministries.

Scholarships and Tuition

Tuition fees for the 2026/2027 academic year are:

Statutory fee: €2,695
Institutional fee: €21,500

The programme offers financial support options, including the MPP Scholarship, which covers the full institutional tuition fee for one outstanding non-EU applicant.

Applicants are encouraged to explore additional funding options such as the Commonwealth Scholarship.

Programme Details

Programme start date: September 1, 2026
Duration: 1 year (full-time)
Location: Maastricht, Netherlands

Application Deadlines

Non-EU/EEA applicants (requiring a visa): May 1, 2026
EU applicants: June 1, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to secure admission and explore scholarship opportunities.

Who Should Apply

The programme welcomes applicants from diverse academic and professional backgrounds, including recent graduates and mid-career professionals.

It is ideal for individuals interested in:

Public policy
International development
Governance and global affairs
Economic and social policy

How to Apply

Interested candidates can learn more about the programme and submit their application through the official Maastricht University portal.

Prospective students are advised to carefully review admission requirements and prepare all necessary documents before applying.

For more Scholarship opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post MSc in Public Policy and Human Development 2026: Study Policy, Development, and Global Change in the Netherlands(Scholarship Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Youth Summit 2026 in Cape Town: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Changemakers Worldwide
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Global Youth Summit 2026 is returning to Cape Town, South Africa, bringing together a new generation of young leaders who are already creating impact in their communities. This fully funded youth leadership programme is designed to equip participants with practical skills, global connections, and access to funding opportunities to scale their ideas. If you […]

The post Global Youth Summit 2026 in Cape Town: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Changemakers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Youth Summit 2026 is returning to Cape Town, South Africa, bringing together a new generation of young leaders who are already creating impact in their communities. This fully funded youth leadership programme is designed to equip participants with practical skills, global connections, and access to funding opportunities to scale their ideas.

If you are passionate about social change and ready to take your work to the next level, this is your opportunity to join one of the most impactful global youth summits.

About the Global Youth Summit 2026

The Global Youth Summit is not your typical conference. It is a hands-on, immersive experience where young changemakers collaborate, learn, and build lasting relationships.

Participants will engage in:

  • Practical skills-building sessions

  • Peer-to-peer learning and collaboration

  • Cross-cultural exchange with global leaders

  • Real-world problem-solving discussions

This summit is designed for individuals who are already actively working in their communities and want to strengthen their impact.

Who Should Apply?

To be eligible for the Global Youth Summit 2026, applicants must:

  • Be aged 21 to 28 years

  • Be actively involved in community impact or social change initiatives

  • Demonstrate leadership and commitment to creating positive change

Even if your project is still developing, you are encouraged to apply. The summit values potential, passion, and purpose.

What You Will Gain

Selected participants will benefit from:

  • Access to a global network of 1,200+ changemakers

  • Opportunities to collaborate with like-minded leaders

  • Exposure to new ideas, tools, and strategies

  • Continued engagement beyond the summit

Most importantly, participants gain confidence, clarity, and connections to grow their initiatives.

Fully Funded Benefits

For selected participants, the Global Youth Summit 2026 offers:

  • Airfare (for fully funded participants)

  • Accommodation

  • Meals throughout the summit

This ensures that financial barriers do not limit participation for deserving candidates.

Funding Options

While the majority of participants receive full funding, there are also:

  • Limited partially funded spots available

  • Applicants who can self-fund their airfare are encouraged to indicate this in their application

All applicants go through the same competitive selection process, regardless of funding type.

Application Process

Applying for the Global Youth Summit 2026 is simple but requires attention to detail.

Steps to Apply:

  • Complete the three-step application process here

  • Ensure all sections are fully submitted

  • Clearly showcase your work, impact, and vision

Important:
Candidates who do not complete all three steps will not be considered.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the Global Youth Summit can be transformative. Many past participants describe it as a turning point in their journey.

You can expect:

  • A practical and energising learning experience

  • Strong peer connections across countries

  • Access to exclusive grant funding pathways

  • A supportive global community that continues beyond the summit

This is more than an event—it is a launchpad for your impact.

Need a Reminder?

A strong application takes time. If you are not ready to apply immediately:

  • Sign up for a reminder email

  • Receive a notification one week before the deadline

  • Submit your application with confidence and clarity

About Global Changemakers

Global Changemakers is an international youth organisation dedicated to supporting young people to create fair, just, and sustainable communities.

Their Impact:

  • Trained 50,000+ young people

  • Reached participants from 180+ countries

  • Funded 500 youth-led projects

  • Impacted over 16 million people globally

They have successfully organised:

  • 14 fully funded Global Youth Summits

  • 4 Virtual Youth Summits

  • 4 Regional Summits across continents

  • Numerous global skills-building programmes

APPLY NOW

The Global Youth Summit 2026 in Cape Town is a fully funded opportunity for young leaders who are ready to grow, connect, and scale their impact.

If you are building something meaningful in your community, this is your moment to step into a global platform, learn from others, and take your work further.

Start your application today and join a global network of changemakers shaping the future.

The post Global Youth Summit 2026 in Cape Town: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Changemakers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
